Summary: Structure: causal chain. Runx2 is required for the specification and commitment of multipotent mesenchymal cells into the osteoblast lineage and is expressed across osteoprogenitors, preosteoblasts, immature and mature osteoblasts, and osteocytes. Mechanistically, Runx2 induces Sp7 expression (yielding Runx2+ Sp7+ preosteoblasts), induces Fgfr2/Fgfr3 and genes in the Hedgehog, Fgf, Wnt, and Pthlh pathways with reciprocal regulation, and depends on Cbfb for enhanced DNA binding and protein stability. Outcome: Runx2 enhances osteoprogenitor proliferation, promotes osteoblast maturation, and in osteoblasts drives expression of major bone matrix genes (Col1a1, Col1a2, Spp1, Ibsp, Bglap/Bglap2), thereby being essential for bone formation; germline Runx2 deletion abolishes intramembranous and endochondral bone.
References: Komori, T. (2024). Regulation of Skeletal Development and Maintenance by Runx2 and Sp7. Int J Mol Sci, 25(18), 10102. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms251810102
